name: Páramos de Burgos Loop
name_original: De Burgos al Páramo de Vivar — circular
description: Road cycling this 30.8-mile circular route from Burgos to Páramo de Vivar offers moderate difficulty and 1678 feet of elevation gain.
region_intro: Road cycling routes around Arlanzon are characterized by diverse landscapes, including the mountainous Sierra de la Demanda and the Arlanzón River valley. The region features the Embalse de Arlanzón, a reservoir offering scenic routes along its banks. Cyclists encounter a mix of forests, such as junipers, oaks, beech, and pine trees, alongside traditional stone villages. The terrain includes both well-paved surfaces and challenging sections with significant elevation changes. Best road cycling routes around Arlanzon The most popular road cycling route is The Monastery of San Pedro Cardeña from Burgos — circular by Modúbar de la Cuesta, a 26.9 miles (43.4 km) trail that takes 1 hour 58 minutes to complete, featuring varied terrain and historical landmarks. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Los Ausines and Monasterio de San Pedro Cardeña — circular from Burgos, a moderate 25.3 miles (40.7 km) path that winds through rural landscapes and past a historic monastery. Local road cyclists also love the From Quintanillas to Mazuelo de Muño — circular from Burgos, a 38.4 miles (61.8 km) trail leading through rural countryside and small villages, often completed in about 2 hours 39 minutes. Road cycling around Arlanzon is defined by mountainous areas, reservoirs, and river valleys. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy routes to challenging climbs. The routes in Arlanzon are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 170 reviews. More than 7,000 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Arlanzon's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available in Arlanzón?
A: Arlanzón offers a wide selection of road cycling routes, with over 160 options available on komoot. These routes cater to various skill levels, including 25 easy, 115 moderate, and 24 difficult trails.

Q: What kind of landscapes can I expect on road cycling routes around Arlanzón?
A: Road cycling in Arlanzón provides diverse scenery, from the mountainous Sierra de la Demanda to the serene Arlanzón River valley. You'll encounter routes alongside the beautiful Embalse de Arlanzón (Arlanzón Reservoir), offering expansive views. The terrain often features forests of junipers, oaks, beech, and pine trees, interspersed with traditional stone villages.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes in Arlanzón?
A: Yes, for experienced riders seeking a challenge, Arlanzón offers routes with significant elevation changes, particularly within the Sierra de la Demanda. A notable difficult route is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056664732/uzquiza-and-arlanzon-reservoirs-sierra-de-mancilla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Úzquiza and Arlanzón Reservoirs - Sierra de Mancilla Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which covers over 109 km with more than 1300 meters of ascent, providing majestic mountain views.

Q: Are there easy or beginner-friendly road cycling routes in Arlanzón?
A: Absolutely. Arlanzón has 25 easy road cycling routes perfect for beginners or those looking for a relaxed ride. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1060490170/mirador-del-castillo-circular-through-the-city-of-burgos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mirador del Castillo — circular through the city of Burgos&lt;/a&gt;, which is just over 24 km long with moderate elevation, offering a pleasant experience.

Q: Can I find circular road cycling routes in Arlanzón?
A: Many road cycling routes in Arlanzón are circular, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular circular options include &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1208915565/the-monastery-of-san-pedro-cardena-from-burgos-circular-by-modubar-de-la-cuesta" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;The Monastery of San Pedro Cardeña from Burgos — circular by Modúbar de la Cuesta&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056789671/los-ausines-and-monasterio-de-san-pedro-cardena-circular-from-burgos" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Los Ausines and Monasterio de San Pedro Cardeña — circular from Burgos&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural landmarks can I see while road cycling in Arlanzón?
A: While cycling, you can explore several natural landmarks. The region is known for its diverse forests and the scenic Embalse de Arlanzón. You might also encounter unique geological formations or expansive views of the Castilian landscape, such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6041261" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Lone Tree on the Horizon&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there historical sites or castles to explore on road cycling routes?
A: Yes, Arlanzón's routes often pass by historical sites and charming villages. You can discover ancient structures like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5581052" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Sotopalacios&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5524538" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mazuelo Tower&lt;/a&gt;. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2729971" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Atapuerca Archaeological Site&lt;/a&gt; is also a significant historical point of interest nearby.

Q: What do other road cyclists think about the routes in Arlanzón?
A: The road cycling routes in Arlanzón are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 170 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, scenic beauty, and the mix of challenging climbs and pleasant stretches.

Q: How long are the typical road cycling routes in Arlanzón?
A: Route lengths vary significantly. You can find shorter rides, like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1208971997/pedrosa-de-rio-urbel-from-santibanez-zarzaguda-circular-by-the-rio-urbel" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pedrosa de río Urbel from Santibáñez-Zarzaguda — circular by the río Urbel&lt;/a&gt; at around 32 km, to much longer, more demanding tours such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1056664732/uzquiza-and-arlanzon-reservoirs-sierra-de-mancilla-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Úzquiza and Arlanzón Reservoirs - Sierra de Mancilla Loop&lt;/a&gt;, which is over 109 km.

Q: Can I cycle along the Arlanzón River?
A: Yes, the Arlanzón River, which originates in the Sierra de la Demanda, offers pleasant routes along its banks. These riverside paths provide a tranquil cycling experience, often with views of the water and surrounding natural beauty.

Q: Are there road cycling routes with parking facilities in Arlanzón?
A: Many road cycling routes in Arlanzón, especially those starting from towns or popular attractions, offer convenient parking options. It's advisable to check the specific route details on komoot for information on starting points and available parking.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Arlanzón?
A: The region's diverse landscapes make it suitable for cycling through much of the year. Spring and autumn generally offer pleasant temperatures and beautiful scenery, with autumn providing particularly picturesque views of the changing foliage in the forests.
tips:
The Church of Our Lady of the Assumption in Tardajos (Burgos) is one of the most emblematic churches on the French Way of St. James, both for its historical significance and its architecture.

It was built in 13th-century Gothic style, although it incorporates Romanesque (12th-century), Renaissance (16th-17th centuries), and Baroque (18th-century) elements. It stands on a hill in the oldest part of the town, giving it a fortress-like appearance.

Fascinating 18th century church in the town of Páramo del Arroyo, with a classicist baroque altarpiece consisting of three streets, and two bodies and an attic. From the vicinity of the church you can see the wind farm "Páramo".

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this cycling route?
faqs.answer: This circular road cycling route usually takes around 2 hours and 15 minutes to complete. This estimate is for the full 49.6-kilometer distance with 510 meters of elevation gain.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route suitable for beginners?
faqs.answer: Rated as a moderate road ride, this route requires a good level of fitness due to its length and cumulative elevation gain. While not overly technical, beginners might find the distance and ascents challenging. It's best suited for cyclists with some experience.

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route features mostly well-paved surfaces, making it ideal for road bikes. You can expect a mix of open landscapes with gentle ascents and descents, typical of the Burgos province. Some sections may be on quiet minor roads, with others potentially on regional roads that generally have good shoulders.

faqs.question: What historical or cultural points of interest can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: The route has a strong connection to the legend of El Cid Campeador. You'll pass through Vivar del Cid, famed as the knight's birthplace. Other highlights include the historic town of Tardajos and the Church of San Vicente Mártir of Páramo del Arroyo. The region is dotted with small, peaceful villages and historical sites.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle the From Burgos to the Páramo de Vivar — circular route?
faqs.answer: The best time to cycle this route is generally during the spring and autumn months when temperatures are milder. Summers can be hot, and winters may bring colder conditions. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: Where can I park to start this cycling route?
faqs.answer: The route starts from Burgos. You can find parking options within the city of Burgos. For specific parking information, it's advisable to check local resources like visitaburgosciudad.es for up-to-date details on city parking facilities.

faqs.question: Is this cycling route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: As a road cycling route, it is generally not recommended for dogs, especially given the distance and potential for traffic on some sections. Dogs are typically better suited for off-road trails where they can run freely and safely.

faqs.question: Do I need any permits or pay entrance fees for this route?
faqs.answer: No, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to cycle this route. It utilizes public roads and paths.

faqs.question: Are there places to find refreshments or amenities along the route?
faqs.answer: While the route passes through small villages, specific amenities like cafes or shops might be limited, especially outside of Burgos and Vivar del Cid. It's recommended to carry sufficient water and snacks, and plan any stops in advance.

faqs.question: Does this route connect with other well-known cycling paths?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route intersects with several other paths. Notably, it covers sections of the Camino Francés - 04 Burgos a León, El Destierro, and the Camino del Cid (Senderista), among others. This offers opportunities to explore further if you wish.

faqs.question: What type of bike is best suited for this route?
faqs.answer: Given the 'racebike' sport type and the description of mostly well-paved surfaces, a road bike is the most suitable choice for this route. Its design is optimized for efficiency and speed on asphalt.

waypoints.name: Church of San Vicente Mártir of Páramo del Arroyo
